item.title: Explain where Opposition would cut $1B in govt expenditure - Prof. Prasad
From military upbringing to rugby captain: Bitila Tawake to share her journey on fijivillage Her-Story tonight at 7pm
item.description: Ahead of the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s historic Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, a special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono will feature captain Bitila Tawake, who will share her journey from growing up in a strict military household to becoming one of Fiji’s leading female rugby players. Tawake reflects on balancing leadership wit
item.content:encoded:
Ahead of the McDonald’s Fiji Drua Women’s historic Super Rugby Women’s home semi-final against the Queensland Reds this weekend, a special episode of fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono will feature captain Bitila Tawake, who will share her journey from growing up in a strict military household to becoming one of Fiji’s leading female rugby players.

Tawake reflects on balancing leadership with being a player, overcoming criticism in a male-dominated sport, missing family milestones in pursuit of her rugby dreams and the importance of inspiring the next generation of female athletes.

Don't miss the interview with Tawake on fijivillage Her-Story with Temalesi Vono, at 7pm tonight.

item.title: ODPP rejects former staff member's allegations, to refer Statutory Declaration to Australian authorities

item.title: Man charged with murder over alleged payment dispute after sexual services
item.description: A 26-year-old man has pleaded not guilty to allegedly murdering a 55-year-old man in Labasa following a dispute over non-payment

item.title: Police attend crash involving taxi and rubbish collection truck
item.description: Police are at the scene of a crash involving a taxi and a rubbish collection truck. It is believed the truck collided with the taxi, which then crashed into a bus shelter.
item.content:encoded:
Police are at the scene of a crash involving a taxi and a rubbish collection truck.

It is believed the truck collided with the taxi, which then crashed into a bus shelter.

Police are yet to confirm the condition of the drivers or any passengers involved.

item.title: New $2M True Fitness gym opens in Laqere to help Fijians live healthier lives, creates 10 jobs
item.description: People living in Suva to Nausori corridor and surrounding communities now have greater access to a modern fitness facility as Grac

item.title: Luxury superyacht Pisces arrives in Lautoka
item.description: Built by Delta Marine in the United States and delivered in 2011, the 46.02-metre (151-foot) superyacht was originally launched under the name Time For Us before being renamed Pisces . The yacht features a modern semi-displacement fiberglass hull and is powered by twin Caterpillar 3512B diesel engines, capable of reaching speeds of up to 16 knots with a comfortable cruising speed of around 12 knot
item.content:encoded:
Built by Delta Marine in the United States and delivered in 2011, the 46.02-metre (151-foot) superyacht was originally launched under the name Time For Us before being renamed Pisces .

The yacht features a modern semi-displacement fiberglass hull and is powered by twin Caterpillar 3512B diesel engines, capable of reaching speeds of up to 16 knots with a comfortable cruising speed of around 12 knots and it also has a cruising range of approximately 2,700 nautical miles.

Designed by renowned exterior designer Jonathan Quinn Barnett, with interiors by Bonville Associates and naval architecture by Roddan Engineering, Pisces combines luxury with performance.

The yacht accommodates up to 11 guests in five beautifully appointed cabins, supported by a professional crew of around 10.

On board, guests enjoy world-class amenities, including a spacious sundeck, beach club, elevator, advanced stabilizers for a smoother voyage, fully air-conditioned interiors, Wi-Fi and satellite communications, and a range of water sports equipment for swimming and diving.

For those seeking the ultimate luxury holiday, Pisces is available for private charter, with weekly rates ranging from about $410,000 to $520,000, excluding fuel, food, port charges, and other operating expenses.

The arrival of Pisces is expected to provide a welcome boost to Lautoka's economy.

During its visit, the yacht and its crew are likely to purchase fuel, fresh produce, marine supplies, and other services from local businesses.

The visit also reinforces Fiji's growing reputation as a premier destination for luxury yachting in the South Pacific.

item.title: Fiji Human Rights Commission opens first Northern office in Labasa
item.description: Good news for the people of the Northern Division as 27 years after its establishment, the Fiji Human Rights and Anti-Discriminati

item.title: Tikoduadua says Fiji's military must never again enter politics as Govt strengthens civilian control
item.description: Defence Minister Pio Tikoduadua says Fiji's military must never again become involved in politics, stressing that its role is to s
